title: Course Schedule
description: You are given an integer numCourses representing the total number of courses you have to take, labeled from 0 to numCourses - 1. You are also given a 2D array prerequisites, where prerequisites[i] = [a, b] indicates that you must take course b before course a. Return true if you can finish all courses. Otherwise, return false.

examples.explanation: You can take course 0 first, then take course 1. Thus, it is possible to finish all courses.
There is a cycle between courses 0 and 1. You cannot finish all courses due to this circular dependency.
You can take the courses in the order of 0, 1, 2, 3, and 4. Hence, it is possible to complete all courses.

constraints: The number of courses numCourses is between 1 and 2000.
The length of the prerequisites array is between 0 and 5000.
hints: Try to represent the courses and their prerequisites as a graph.
Consider using depth-first search to detect cycles in the graph.
Think about topological sorting and its properties.
Use a coloring technique to mark visited nodes and detect cycles.
